statement.en: From Research Question to Scientific Workflow: Leveraging Agentic AI for Science Automation
statement.summary: Scientific workflow systems automate execution -- scheduling, fault tolerance, resource management -- but not the semantic translation that precedes it. Scientists still manually convert research questions into workflow specifications, a task requiring both domain knowledge and infrastructure expertise. We propose an agentic architecture that closes this gap through three layers: an LLM interprets natural language into structured intents (semantic layer); validated generators produce reproducible workflow DAGs (deterministic layer); and domain experts author ``Skills'': markdown documents encoding vocabulary mappings, parameter constraints, and optimization strategies (knowledge layer). This decomposition confines LLM non-determinism to intent extraction: identical intents always yield ide

proposer: Bartosz Balis, Michal Orzechowski, Piotr Kica et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The paper presents a provisional architectural framework (agentic AI for workflow automation) that is plausible and technically coherent, but relies on implementation details not yet validated at scale. The three-layer decomposition (semantic/deterministic/knowledge) is theoretically sound but empirically unproven in the abstract. The claim about 'confining LLM non-determinism' is a design aspiration, not an established fact. This is a preprint (2026-04-25) advancing a novel systems approach; it merits FLOWING rather than TRUE (not yet peer-validated) or NEITHER (it makes concrete testable claims).
